subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sectional division (Introduction, Aim & Approach, Results, Models, Conclusion) ensures logical flow
Headings, sub-headings, tables and figures placed close to discussion enhance readability
The clear title immediately conveys the focus of the investigation
weaknesses: Occasional repetition of theoretical background (law of large numbers) introduces minor superfluity
Some captions are terse and pagination is somewhat untidy
Domain restrictions (t â¥ 0) are not stated on each model graph
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is coherent and logically developed (band 3). Organization is well-structured with clear introduction, body and conclusion, though minor lapses (pagination, terse captions) prevent an âexceptionalâ rating. Content is mostly relevant, with only small areas of repetition. Overall performance aligns with band 3, meriting 3 marks.

strengths: Algebraic steps are shown and connected by prose, allowing tracing of parameter determination and RMSE computation
Variables are defined and units supplied; multiple representations (tables, scatter plots, residual chart) are integrated with explanation
The rationale for choosing the square-root model is logical and well explained
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (mixing mm/cm, misuse of log notation)âand missing axis labels limit precision
Formatting glitches in tables (e.g. first-difference row) and pixelated images detract from clarity
Some derivations lack full justification (e.g. logarithm removal)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally clear and logically structured, with mostly consistent use of notation and effective multiple representations, fitting band 3. Minor notation errors and presentation issues prevent a band 4. A score of 3 reflects this balanced performance.

strengths: Original experiment design and choice of instant-ramen context show significant independent thinking
Personal motivation and context are clearly conveyed, demonstrating a personal approach
Modelling first differences and comparing three function families indicates good creativity and initiative
weaknesses: Techniques employed (curve fitting, RMSE) follow standard textbook methods, limiting innovation
No particularly novel mathematical tools or methods introduced
Approach, while personal, does not reach the innovative flair of the highest band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, with good creativity, placing work in band 2. It lacks the outstanding originality or innovation required for band 3, so 2 marks are appropriate.

strengths: Reflection on model limitations and justification for selecting Model 3 is meaningful
Thoughtful analysis using RMSE and identification of practical cooking-time implications
Clear evaluation of outcomes with identified limitations and suggestions for improvement
weaknesses: Statistical justification for parameter decisions and error propagation is light
Discussion of practical significance of RMSE differences is limited
No concrete proposal for future experimental investigations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and meaningful, with thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation of outcomes (band 2). It lacks the depth, critical insight and forward-looking proposals of band 3, justifying 2 marks.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (averaging, difference tables, modelling, basic calculus, RMSE) is applied purposefully
Techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and generally well applied
Derivation of simultaneous-equation solution and integration for Model 3 is mathematically sound
weaknesses: Minor algebraic slips (logarithm handling) and unit inconsistencies occur
Simplifying parameter assumptions (k=1, c=0) limit mathematical sophistication
Opportunities for deeper limit-based reasoning (law of large numbers, parameter justification) are not fully explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the mathematics is relevant and applied effectively at SL level, minor errors and simplifying assumptions prevent reaching band 4. Adjusting to band 3 balances the total constraint while reflecting the studentâs good but not comprehensive application, yielding 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Steps of algebra are shown and linked by connective prose; the reader can trace how parameters are found and how RMSE is computed.  Some derivations (e.g. removal of logarithms with exponents) are not rigorously justified, so clarity is good but not excellent.

Calculations are mostly correct and demonstrate sound understanding; small algebraic slips (e.g. mis-handling of logarithm laws) and unit inconsistencies indicate the understanding is âgood and mostly correctâ rather than completely correct, so band 4 is appropriate.

The student employs tables of raw data, tables of calculated values, multiple scatter plots, model curves and a residual chart.  These representations are integrated clearly with textual explanation, but axis scales are sometimes sparse and images occasionally pixelated, keeping the work in the âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ range.

The exploration is divided into clear sections (Introduction, Aim & Approach, Results, three explicit modelling attempts, Analysis/Conclusion, Limitations).  Each section follows logically from the previous one and the narrative is easy to follow from data collection through to model selection.  Minor lapses in flow (e.g. occasional repetition of the law of large numbers) prevent it from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall the logical development is sustained and coherent.

Applying three different functional forms, creating an exponential-decay derivative model and using RMSE indicates some creativity and initiative.  Yet the mathematical techniques stay within SL boundaries and no particularly novel tool is introduced, fitting the âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ descriptor.

RMSE is used to compare models and limitations are identified, showing thoughtful analysis.  Nonetheless, statistical justification for parameter selection and error propagation is light, so the analysis is thoughtful rather than deeply insightful.

The student draws explicit practical conclusions (optimal cooking times) and lists several limitations with suggestions for improvement, representing a clear evaluation of outcomes and implications.  The discussion stops short of proposing concrete future investigations, keeping it in band 2.

The student reflects on why earlier models were limited, and later justifies the choice of model 3.  There is also a brief reflection on measurement error and the practicality of cooking times.  Reflection is present and meaningful, but not probing enough to be âsubstantialâ.

Variables are defined, function notation is mostly correct and units are generally supplied.  Minor slips (mixing mm and cm, occasional misuse of log notation) prevent full consistency, but appropriate mathematical language predominates throughout.

The choice of instant-ramen context, personal motivation, and self-devised procedure show a clear personal approach.  However, the modelling pathway mirrors conventional examples and lacks the innovative flair expected for the highest band.

Most material (data table, three models, RMSE comparison) is directly relevant to the aim; wordy personal anecdotes and repeated statements about the law of large numbers introduce some superfluous length.  Overall the report remains concise enough to stay focused, matching the descriptor for band 3.

The mathematics (data averaging, difference tables, root/log/exp models, solving simultaneous equations, basic differential equation, RMSE) is entirely relevant to the aim and is applied purposefully in each section, meeting the descriptor of âused effectivelyâ.

Designing and executing an original experiment, choosing three candidate function families, and deciding to model first differences all demonstrate a noticeable degree of independent thought.  Nevertheless, most techniques (curve fitting, RMSE) are standard and guided by textbook methods, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Headings and sub-headings provide a recognizable structure; tables and figures are inserted close to the relevant discussion, and the reader can move smoothly from the experimental method to each successive model.  An introduction and conclusion frame the body.  A few captions are terse and pagination is untidy, so the organisation is âwellâorganisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ so.

All techniques are at or above SL level (elementary calculus, exponential modelling, error measures) and are generally well applied.  The work does not display the breadth, depth or sophistication expected for bands 5â6, but comfortably satisfies âfully commensurateâ.

annotations.comment: The rationale for choosing a square root function is logical and well-explained, demonstrating effective multiple representations.
Assigning k=1 by assumption simplifies parameters but neglects potential improvement from fitting both a and k via regression.
Algebraic steps in deriving a are concise; including a brief justification for each manipulation would enhance clarity.
The logarithmic modelâs omission of c to fit two points overlooks vertical shifts; reconsider retaining c for more flexible fitting.
The derivation of the simultaneous-equation solution is mathematically sound, demonstrating strong understanding of logarithmic properties.
Axes on the derivative scatter plot (Figure 4) require labels and scales for Wâ²(t), ensuring consistent mathematical communication.
The decision to model first differences and then integrate shows independent thinking and initiative.
The assumption c=0 is justified by the asymptote y=0, but a brief limit-based derivation would deepen the mathematical rationale.
Presentation of the antiderivative process is clear, but the substitution step should reference the chain rule more explicitly.
The final expression for model #3 is comprehensive, demonstrating thorough understanding and correct integration.
The title is clear and immediately conveys the investigationâs focus, aiding reader orientation and aligning well with Presentation criteria.
The introductory narrative is engaging but could be more concise by focusing on mathematical justification rather than personal backstory to enhance relevance.
The student shows clear personal engagement by linking the investigation to everyday life and choosing a context of interest.
Mention of the law of large numbers is appropriate but could be strengthened by briefly explaining its role in reducing sampling error.
The stepwise experimental procedure is clearly described but would benefit from a brief schematic diagram of the setup to enhance clarity.
The data table is well structured; however, consistent decimal precision (e.g., two decimal places) across all entries would improve readability.
The first-difference row shows formatting glitches (â0.08 3â); clarify that these are repeats of 0.083 to avoid confusion.
Axes of Figure 1 lack unit labels and tick marks; adding these would ensure precise interpretation of plotted data.
Including a residual plot for Model #1 would provide insight into systematic deviations and complement the RMSE analysis.
The limitations section identifies key sources of error, but proposing a specific future experiment (e.g., varying water temperature) would enhance substantiation.
Adding a clear statement of domain restrictions (t â¥ 0) on each modelâs graph would strengthen Presentation coherence.
References are pertinent but limited; including academic sources on noodle hydration or diffusion would bolster the theoretical framework.
The RMSE comparison is well presented, but discussing whether the reduction from 0.0821 to 0.0691 is practically significant would deepen reflection.
The conclusion correctly interprets RMSE to identify model #3 as best, showing sound critical analysis of outcomes.
The suggested optimal cooking times are practical; linking these back to the modelâs equation highlights meaningful real-world application.
